intro: The skull is a collection of 22 bones that protect the brain and sense organs.
notes: The Cranium is the 'brain box' that protects the brain.
The Mandible (lower jaw) is the ONLY movable bone in the skull.
Sutures are immovable joints that look like zig-zag lines.
The Orbit is the cavity that protects the eyes.

hotspots.label: Cranium
hotspots.description:
The Cranium is the dome-shaped part of the skull. It is made of 8 flat bones fused together.

Its main function is to enclose and protect the delicate brain tissue.
hotspots.examTip: The bones of the cranium are joined by Sutures (immovable joints).

hotspots.label: Sutures
hotspots.description: Sutures are the zig-zag lines found on the skull. They are fibrous joints that do not allow any movement.
hotspots.examTip: They are a perfect example of Fixed (Immovable) Joints.

hotspots.label: Orbit
hotspots.description: The Orbit is the deep, bony cavity that holds the eye. It protects the eyeball from physical damage.
hotspots.examTip: The back of the orbit has a hole for the Optic Nerve to pass through to the brain.

hotspots.label: Mandible
hotspots.description: The Mandible holds the lower teeth. It moves up and down to allow chewing (mastication) and speaking.
hotspots.examTip: Important Question: Which is the ONLY movable bone in the skull? Answer: The Mandible.

hotspots.label: Maxilla
hotspots.description: The Maxilla holds the upper teeth. Unlike the lower jaw, it is fused to the cranium and does not move.
hotspots.examTip: It forms part of the nose and the roof of the mouth (palate).
